The Ultimate Guide: How Long Does It Take for
Lip Flip to Work?

By Aesthetic Science Team
Medically Reviewed

"While fillers provide volume, the lip flip provides character. Understanding the timeline of muscle relaxation is essential for managing your expectations and achieving the perfect pout."

What You'll Learn

• The Day-by-Day Timeline• Botox vs Dysport Units• Flip vs. Filler Comparison• The Golden Rules of Aftercare• National Cost Averages• Longevity & Reversibility

The Rise of the "Subtle" Lip

If you've been browsing aesthetic trends lately, you've undoubtedly encountered the term "Lip Flip." Unlike the pillowy, voluminous results associated with traditional dermal fillers, the lip flip is celebrated for its extreme subtlety. It is the procedure of choice for those who want their lips to look "better," but not necessarily "bigger."

But here is the most common question patients ask in the injector's chair: "How long for a lip flip to work?" If you have a date, a wedding, or a photoshoot, understanding the "flipping" window is critical.

What Exactly Does a Lip Flip Do?

Before we dive into the timing, we must understand the mechanics. Many people confuse this with filler, but what is a lip flip vs fillers? The difference lies in the substance.

A Botox Lip Flip involves the precise injection of a neurotoxin—usually Botox Cosmetic, Dysport, or Xeomin—into the orbicularis oris muscle. This is the circular muscle that surrounds your mouth and allows you to pucker, whistle, and speak.

The "Flip" Effect

By injecting small amounts of toxin into the upper border of this muscle, the injector causes the muscle to relax. This relaxation allows the upper lip to "roll" or "flip" slightly upward and outward. It doesn't add volume; it reveals more of the pink part (the vermilion border) that was already there.

Gummy Smile Correction

One of the biggest benefits of a lip flip is for those with a "gummy smile." When you smile, the relaxed muscle prevents the upper lip from thinning out or curling under too much, hiding the gums and creating a more balanced aesthetic.

How Long to See Lip Flip Results: The Timeline

Science-Backed Data

Because neurotoxins take time to block the neurotransmitters (acetylcholine) that signal muscle movement, results are never instant. Here is exactly how long a lip flip takes to show, broken down day by day:

1
Hour 1 - 24

Injection & Recovery

You will feel exactly the same. You might have tiny red marks at the injection points. Some patients experience very minor swelling, but most people can return to work immediately. This is NOT the result phase.

2
Day 2 - 3

Molecular Binding

The neurotoxin is currently busy binding to the nerve endings. You may feel a slight 'heaviness' in your upper lip. If you're asking 'does lip flip work?' and looking in the mirror today, be patient. The muscle is only just beginning to weaken.

3
Day 4 - 7

The Initial Flip

This is when most people start to see the change. <strong>How long for a lip flip to kick in?</strong> Usually right here. You'll notice your smile looks a bit different in photos, and the upper lip doesn't disappear when you laugh. It's the 'aha!' moment.

4
Day 10 - 14

Peak & Final Result

<strong>How long does it take for a lip flip to show in full?</strong> Fourteen days. At this two-week mark, the muscle relaxation is total. This is the time to assess your final look and see if you need any 'touch-ups' (though touch-ups are rare for this procedure).

Stop Guessing.
See your results now.

Wondering if a lip flip will actually suit your face? Our AI engine simulates the exact movement of your vermilion border instantly.

Free for first-time users

Units Demystified: Botox vs. Dysport

One of the most confusing parts of getting a lip flip is the unit count. How many units in a lip flip? It depends entirely on the brand of neurotoxin your injector uses.

Botox Cosmetic

2 - 4 units

Botox is highly concentrated. Most injectors use 2-4 units total, placed in 4 injection points along the vermilion border.

Dysport

5 - 10 units

How many units of dysport for lip flip? Dysport units are measured differently (it's less concentrated). You need about 2.5x to 3x more units to get the same results as Botox.

Note: How many botox units for lip flip can vary based on your muscle strength. Men or people with very strong lip muscles may require 5-6 units.

What NOT To Do After a Lip Flip

You've spent the time and money—don't ruin your results in the first 24 hours. Neurotoxin migration is a real risk if you don't follow the "Golden Rules." If the toxin moves to the wrong part of the muscle, it can affect your speech or even make your smile look crooked. Check out our Complete Lip Flip Aftercare Guide for an in-depth survival guide on what to avoid.

Can I eat after a lip flip?

Yes, but avoid foods that require intense chewing or messy eating for at least 6 hours. Try not to wipe your mouth aggressively. Can you eat after lip flip? Absolutely, just be gentle.

Can you kiss after lip flip?

Skip the heavy puckering or passionate kissing for the first 24-48 hours. Any intense pressure on the injection site can cause the product to migrate prematurely.

The 24-Hour Checklist

  • No Straws: The suction can cause migration.
  • No Rubbing: Don't touch the injection sites.
  • Stay Upright: Don't lie down for 4 hours.
  • No Alcohol: Alcohol thins the blood and increases bruising.
  • Skip the Gym: Intense blood flow can flush the toxin.
  • No Hot Tubs/Saunas: Heat can cause inflammation.

Lip Flip vs. Lip Filler: A Deep Dive

Still confused about what's the difference between a lip flip and lip filler? Think of it this way: Filler is for size; Flip is for shape. To see exactly how these two treatments compare in longevity, mechanism, and cost, read our Lip Flip vs. Filler Deep Dive.

CategoryThe Lip FlipLip Filler (Juvederm/Restylane)
Main GoalReveal hidden surface areaAdd volume and hydrate
How long to work?3 - 14 DaysInstant (with swelling)
Average Cost$80 - $200$500 - $1,200
Longevity3 - 4 Months6 - 12 Months
Pain Level1/10 (Quick pinches)4/10 (Requires numbing)

How Much is a Lip Flip?

One of the biggest selling points of the lip flip is the price. How much does lip flip cost compared to other treatments? In 2026, the national average remains remarkably affordable.

Most clinics charge either by the unit or a flat "procedure fee."

  • Small Boutique Clinics: $80 - $150
  • High-End Plastic Surgery Centers: $200 - $350
  • Per Unit Pricing: $12 - $20 per unit of Botox.

Expert Insight: If a clinic is charging $20 for a lip flip, beware. They may be over-diluting the product. A safe, high-quality treatment should reflect the skill and the product cost.

Is Lip Flip Permanent?

For anyone nervous about commitment: Is a lip flip permanent? No.

Neurotoxins are naturally metabolized by your body over time. In the case of the lips, because we use them constantly (talking, eating, smiling), the toxin tends to wear off faster than it does in the forehead or 'crow's feet.'

How often to get lip flip?

To maintain the result, you should plan to visit your injector every 10 to 12 weeks. If you wait longer, the muscle will fully regain its strength and the "flip" will disappear.

Can you reverse a lip flip?

Unlike fillers, which can be dissolved with an enzyme called hyaluronidase, neurotoxins cannot be reversed. Once it's injected, you have to wait for it to wear off. However, because the dose is so small (2-4 units), if you dislike the result, it will significantly fade within 4-6 weeks anyway.

Common Lip Flip Questions

Should I get a lip flip?

You are a great candidate if your upper lip "disappears" when you smile, or if you want a subtle improvement without the "fake" look of fillers. It's also great for those looking for an affordable introduction to aesthetics.

What is Botox lip flip vs Dysport?

Dysport tends to "spread" slightly more and sometimes kicks in a day or two faster (day 2-3). Botox is more localized. Both provide identical aesthetic results in the hands of a skilled injector.

Where to get a lip flip near me?

Always choose a board-certified dermatologist, plastic surgeon, or a registered nurse injector with specialized training in facial anatomy. Don't go to a "beauty bar" that lacks medical supervision.

Conclusion: The 14-Day Rule

Managing expectations is the key to happiness in cosmetic treatments. If you are asking how long does it take for a lip flip to work, remember the 14-day rule. Do not judge your results on day 3. Do not panic on day 5. Wait for the full two-week window for the magic to happen.

The lip flip is the ultimate "everyone will notice, but no one will know" treatment. It enhances your natural beauty by allowing your own anatomy to shine a little brighter.
